verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY after work with a 16:30 start at Rey Cortiço is the best of what’s available, but it’s pretty borderline. The wind looks like it’s on the wrong side for the spot and Surfline consistency isn’t great, so I’d check the Surfline cams and the MEO Beachcam before heading out—if it doesn’t look clearly cleaner than expected, I’d skip the drive. If the timing lines up with the rising tide and the banks are firing, you might get those more punchy peaks rather than messy water. Either way, cam check first to decide.

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 50 kJ, 7 s period, lully 1/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 42 kJ, 8 s period, lully 1/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 31 kJ, 7 s period, lully 6/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and weak over the Baleal sandbanks.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and weak over the Baleal sandbanks; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size; Baleal may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide makes this one less convincing.

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 52 kJ, 7 s period, lully 14/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay; Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cantinho da Baia may feel too soft without more push; SwellMap rating 2/10 Poor — caution.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.
